scope_note: QA Chebotarev Density for Q(sqrt 5)/Q cert [425]. Primary sources: Dirichlet, G.L. (1837) 'Beweis des Satzes, dass jede unbegrenzte arithmetische Progression, deren erstes Glied und Differenz ganze Zahlen ohne gemeinschaftlichen Factor sind, unendlich viele Primzahlen enthaelt' Abhandlungen der Kgl. Preussischen Akademie der Wissenschaften pp. 45-81 (L-functions for arithmetic progressions; L(1,chi)!=0 for non-principal chi; equidistribution of primes by residue class); Chebotarev, N. (1926) 'Die Bestimmung der Dichtigkeit einer Menge von Primzahlen welche zu einer gegebenen Substitutionsklasse gehoeren' Mathematische Annalen 95 pp. 191-228 doi:10.1007/BF01453016 (Frobenius equidistribution theorem; density of primes with Frobenius class C = |C|/|Gal(L/K)|; for Q(sqrt5)/Q both classes have weight 1/2); Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) pp. 525-532 doi:10.2307/2309169 (rank of apparition; Fibonacci structure mod p; foundational for [416]-[424] chain); Pearson, K. (1900) 'On the criterion that a given system of deviations from the probable in the case of a correlated system of variables is such that it can be reasonably supposed to have arisen from random sampling' Philosophical Magazine 50 pp. 157-175 (chi-squared goodness-of-fit test; df=3 for 4 residue classes; critical 11.345 at alpha=0.01). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): (A) chi_5=(*/5) Legendre/Kronecker symbol mod 5 is completely multiplicative: chi_5(mn)=chi_5(m)*chi_5(n) for gcd(mn,5)=1; verified 200 pairs; (B) among primes p in [7,10000], residue classes {1,2,3,4} mod 5 each contain ~1/4 of the primes; chi^2(df=3)=0.069 < 11.345 (alpha=0.01); (C) split fraction #{split p<=N}/#{p<=N,p>5} within 0.03 of 0.5 for N=1000,5000,10000; (D) partial Dirichlet series sum_{n=1}^{10000} chi_5(n)/n = 0.430409 matches class number formula 2*log(phi)/sqrt(5)=0.430409 to 6 decimal places; witnesses L(1,chi_5)!=0. GALOIS STRUCTURE: Gal(Q(sqrt5)/Q)=Z/2Z={id,sigma} where sigma: sqrt5->-sqrt5; Frob_p=id (split, cert [423]) or sigma (inert, cert [424]); Chebotarev: density = |class|/|Z/2Z| = 1/2 for each; split=inert=1/2. THEOREM NT: QA layer: chi_5(n) from n%5 (pure integer); prime residue counts (pure integer). Observer layer: chi-squared test, ratio fractions, L-series partial sum (float, lawful). CLASS NUMBER FORMULA: L(1,chi_5)=2*log(epsilon)/sqrt(D) for real quadratic Q(sqrt5): D=5 (discriminant), epsilon=(1+sqrt5)/2=phi (fundamental unit), h=1 (class number); gives 2*log(phi)/sqrt(5)~0.4304. L(1,chi_5)!=0 because log(phi)!=0 (phi>1 transcendental). CHAIN: [416]->[417]->[418]->[419]->[420]->[421]->[422]->[423]->[424]->[425] closes GL_1/Q(sqrt5) picture; next rung = GL_2 (symmetric square, Rankin-Selberg, or Sato-Tate). 4 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-15.
